meta.intent: Redesign the form-builder product page. Tone: technical, user-first, minimal marketing fluff. Goal: enterprise B2B buyers + IT decision makers.

amazon_q_prompt.task: You are helping redesign a product landing page for an enterprise form builder (ivaflow.com/en/form-builder). I have 10 sections of content data below. For EACH section: (1) look at the existing page builder templates/sections available in the current site builder and suggest which existing section/block best fits. (2) For sections marked 'needs_new_section: true', design a brand-new custom section and provide its complete JSON configuration with all content filled in, ready to paste into the page builder. (3) Walk me through each section one at a time and wait for my approval before moving to the next. Do NOT skip or summarize — every field in the data must appear in the output.
notes: Section 05 (Multi-step / Wizard) needs a new section because no standard template shows progressive step UI + form-in-form concept well.
Section 10 (Voice & Mobile) needs a new section because IVR voice form filling is a unique differentiator with no template equivalent.
All other sections should map to existing page builder blocks where possible.
Preserve every badge, chip, feature item, and table row — do not omit or summarize.

sections.suggested_block_type: Full-width hero with headline, subheadline, badge chips, dual CTA buttons, and a product screenshot/mockup on the right

content.headline: Build any form or data structure — zero code required
content.subheadline: From a simple contact form to a multi-step enterprise data model with conditional logic, API connections, and approval workflows. One visual designer handles it all.

media.description: Visual designer canvas showing a multi-column form with fields being dragged, mobile preview panel visible on the right side

sections.copy_notes: Lead with what the product does, not what it is. Avoid words like 'enterprise-grade', 'powerful', 'cutting-edge'. Users scan hero in under 3 seconds — headline + 2 chips + 1 CTA is enough. The unique angle vs competitors: this builder handles both simple forms AND complex data structures — that breadth is the hook.

sections.suggested_block_type: Two-column feature block: text/feature list on left, animated product screenshot or interactive demo on right

content.headline: Design the form exactly as it should look
content.subheadline: Drag fields onto the canvas, resize them, arrange multi-column layouts. Preview on desktop, tablet, or mobile before publishing.
content.body_copy: Unlike code-based form builders, changes render instantly in the same view. No save-and-refresh cycle. Start from a blank canvas or a template, apply your brand colors, font, and logo in one settings panel.

feature_items.title: Live preview
feature_items.description: See desktop, tablet, and mobile simultaneously while editing.

feature_items.title: Multi-column layout
feature_items.description: Place fields side by side, not just stacked vertically.

feature_items.title: Brand kit
feature_items.description: Logo, colors, fonts applied globally across all forms in one settings panel.

feature_items.title: Custom CSS class
feature_items.description: Assign a CSS class to any field or container for advanced styling without touching the core builder.

feature_items.title: Starter templates
feature_items.description: Pre-built layouts for HR, Finance, IT, Sales, and Ops workflows ready to customize.

feature_items.title: Undo / version history
feature_items.description: Non-destructive editing — roll back any change at any time.

media.description: Canvas editor showing drag-and-drop in progress, with responsive preview panel toggling between desktop and mobile views

sections.copy_notes: Key differentiator vs Typeform: multi-column layouts are not possible in Typeform's one-question-per-page model. Key differentiator vs Jotform: cleaner canvas UX — Jotform's editor can feel cluttered. Emphasize the 'see it instantly' angle — no publish-to-preview friction.

sections.suggested_block_type: Centered headline + subheadline, then a categorized grid or table of field types, optionally with icon per category

content.headline: 30+ field types — one library for every data shape
content.subheadline: Text, numbers, dates, files, signatures, maps, lookups, calculated values, and embedded sub-forms. Every field is configurable: label, placeholder, help text, validation rules, and visibility conditions.

media.description: Screenshot of the field picker sidebar showing categorized field type icons

sections.copy_notes: The Communication category (IVR voice field, SMS trigger, Fax) is unique to IVA — no competitor offers these as native form fields. Flag these visually with a 'Unique' or 'IVA exclusive' badge in the UI.

sections.suggested_block_type: Two-column block: feature list on one side, logic rule builder screenshot or diagram on the other

content.headline: Show only what's relevant — hide the rest
content.subheadline: Define rules on any field: show/hide, enable/disable, set required/optional, pre-fill values — all based on previous answers, user role, or the current workflow stage.
content.body_copy: Rules are built in a visual editor, not YAML or code. Complex branching with multiple conditions using AND/OR operators is fully supported. Logic can reference fields across different sections or even across linked forms.

feature_items.title: Show / hide fields
feature_items.description: Conditionally render any individual field or entire form sections based on user input.

feature_items.title: Dynamic required
feature_items.description: Make a field required only when a specific condition is met, not always.

feature_items.title: Dependent dropdowns
feature_items.description: Cascade select options based on the value of a prior field — e.g. Country → City → District.

feature_items.title: Workflow-stage aware logic
feature_items.description: Show different fields depending on the current stage in the BPMS workflow (e.g. draft vs review vs approved).

feature_items.title: Computed default values
feature_items.description: Pre-fill a field automatically using a formula result or a value fetched from an external API.

media.description: Visual rule builder showing conditions: IF field A = value THEN show field B, with AND/OR toggles visible

sections.copy_notes: Typeform supports basic skip logic only. Jotform supports multi-condition logic. IVA's differentiator is workflow-stage awareness — the form's visible fields change based on where it sits in a BPMS process, not just user input. This is a feature no standalone form builder can match.

new_section_design_brief.layout: Full-width section with a horizontal step-progress illustration at top (3-5 steps shown as numbered nodes connected by a line), followed by a two-column layout: feature list left, animated wizard demo or screenshot right. The step progress visual should be custom-built, not a generic progress bar.
new_section_design_brief.visual_concept: Show a form being filled step by step — step 1 complete (checkmark), step 2 active (highlighted), steps 3-4 greyed out. Below the steps, show a nested/repeatable sub-form row to illustrate 'form-in-form'.
new_section_design_brief.unique_selling_point: Form-in-form (nested repeatable sub-forms) — highlight this as a named feature, not just a bullet. It's the key differentiator for enterprise data collection with repeating records.

content.headline: Break long forms into guided steps — with validation at every stage
content.subheadline: Split any form into wizard pages with a progress bar. Validate each step before advancing. Embed repeatable sub-forms inside any step for complex data structures.
content.body_copy: Completion rates drop sharply on single-page forms with more than 8 fields. Wizard mode shows one step at a time with validation gates between pages — invalid data never reaches the next step. For enterprise use cases, form-in-form technology lets you embed a fully repeatable sub-form inside any step, eliminating the need to re-design shared field groups.

feature_items.title: Page-level validation
feature_items.description: Block the 'Next' button until all required fields on the current step pass validation rules.

feature_items.title: Progress indicator
feature_items.description: Step counter or progress bar — style and label text are fully configurable per form.

feature_items.title: Conditional step routing
feature_items.description: Skip entire pages based on earlier answers — users never see steps irrelevant to them.

feature_items.title: Form-in-form (nested sub-forms)
feature_items.description: Embed a fully repeatable sub-form inside any step. Add/remove rows dynamically. Avoids re-building shared field sets across multiple forms.

feature_items.title: Save & resume
feature_items.description: Users can save a draft mid-wizard and return later to complete it — ideal for lengthy approval or onboarding forms.

feature_items.title: Per-step help text
feature_items.description: Add section-level instructions, images, or video to any step to guide users through complex inputs.

media.description: Multi-step form showing step 1 complete, step 2 active with fields, steps 3-4 pending. Below a step, a nested repeatable table sub-form with Add Row button visible.

sections.copy_notes: Form-in-form is the anchor feature here — give it a named callout card or a highlighted box, not just a bullet point. Neither Typeform nor basic Jotform plans support nested repeatable sub-forms. This is the key enterprise differentiator for HR, procurement, and operations teams.

sections.suggested_block_type: Three-column or four-column icon + title + description card grid, or a horizontal event-flow diagram showing Trigger → Condition → Action

content.headline: What happens after submit is as important as the form itself
content.subheadline: Trigger actions on submit, update, or field-change events. Send notifications, create records, start approval workflows, or call external APIs — all configured without writing code.

feature_items.title: On-submit actions
feature_items.description: Email or SMS notification, record creation in CRM or ERP, API call, or BPMS workflow start — triggered immediately on form submission.

feature_items.title: On-field-change events
feature_items.description: Recalculate a running total, fetch a lookup value from an external system, or display a contextual message the moment a specific field is changed.

feature_items.title: Real-time notifications
feature_items.description: Push alerts to email, SMS, fax, or system notification channel simultaneously on submission.

feature_items.title: Webhook / external API call
feature_items.description: POST the full form payload (or a subset of fields) to any external HTTPS endpoint on any event.

feature_items.title: Approval routing
feature_items.description: Route submission to one or more reviewers; form re-opens for correction if a reviewer rejects — with comments attached.

feature_items.title: Payment trigger
feature_items.description: Initiate a payment gateway transaction (e.g. Stripe, PayPal) as a form submission action.

event_flow_diagram.description: Visual: Event (Submit / Field Change / Update) → Condition (optional: IF field X = Y) → Action (Notify / Create Record / Call API / Start Workflow / Request Payment)

media.description: Screenshot of the event/action configuration panel showing a submit event triggering an email notification and a webhook call

sections.copy_notes: This section bridges the form builder to the broader BPMS platform — use this to reinforce that IVA forms are not standalone but part of an end-to-end process. The event flow diagram (Trigger → Condition → Action) is the most important visual here.

sections.suggested_block_type: Logo grid / hub-and-spoke integration diagram + categorized table below, or tabs by category

content.headline: Forms that talk to the rest of your stack
content.subheadline: Pre-populate fields from CRM, ERP, or any REST API. Push submissions into project tools, accounting systems, file storage, or e-signature platforms. Bi-directional sync, not just one-way export.

integration_table.examples: Any REST endpoint via webhook or API field
IVA workflow engine, IVA dashboard, IVA CRM
integration_table.direction: Deep native integration — not via connector

media.description: Central node labeled 'IVA Form' with spokes connecting to: CRM, ERP, Email, File Storage, E-signature, Accounting, Webhooks, BPMS

sections.copy_notes: The key differentiator here is bi-directional: forms can READ from master data (e.g. pull a product list from ERP into a dropdown) AND write back on submit. Jotform and Typeform mostly export/push only. The BPMS native integration row should be visually highlighted as different from third-party connectors.

sections.suggested_block_type: Two-column block with feature cards on left and a dashboard/report screenshot on right, or a tab-based feature showcase

content.headline: Data collected is only useful if you can act on it
content.subheadline: Explore submissions in a live table view. Build pivot reports, charts, and KPI widgets. Export to XLS, CSV, or PDF — or embed dashboards in internal portals.
content.body_copy: Unlike Google Forms (basic spreadsheet export) or Typeform (response view with drop-off rates), IVA's reporting engine is part of the same BPMS platform — submission data joins with workflow, approval, and process data in one unified reporting layer.

feature_items.title: Custom views
feature_items.description: Table view, gallery view, and spreadsheet-like grid. Filter and sort by any field. Save views for reuse.

feature_items.title: Advanced formula columns
feature_items.description: Add calculated columns inside the data table — aggregations, conditionals, and mathematical formulas on submission data.

feature_items.title: Chart builder
feature_items.description: Pie, bar, line, and pivot charts — all built directly from submission data, no export required.

feature_items.title: Drop-off analysis
feature_items.description: For multi-step forms: see exactly which step users abandon, and how long they spend on each page.

feature_items.title: Export
feature_items.description: XLS, CSV, and PDF export with custom column selection and date range filters.

feature_items.title: Embedded dashboards
feature_items.description: Embed report widgets in a portal page, intranet, or external site via iframe or API.

media.description: Dashboard view showing a bar chart of submissions over time, a pivot table of department-level responses, and a KPI card for total submissions this month

sections.copy_notes: Typeform is the only major competitor with question-level drop-off analysis — IVA should highlight this as a parity feature when positioning against Typeform in enterprise analytics conversations. The BPMS unified data angle (form data + process data in one report) is a structural advantage no standalone form builder can match.

sections.suggested_block_type: Dark or neutral background section with icon + title + description cards in a 3-column grid, plus a security badge row (SSL, GDPR, etc.)

content.headline: Field-level access control, audit trails, and encryption
content.subheadline: Role-based permissions control who sees, fills, edits, or approves each form. Every submission and field-level change is logged in an immutable audit trail.

feature_items.title: Role-based access control
feature_items.description: Separate permission levels for form designer, form filler, reviewer, and administrator — per form and per field.

feature_items.title: Field-level visibility permissions
feature_items.description: Hide sensitive fields from specific roles — e.g. salary fields hidden from non-HR reviewers in the same form.

feature_items.title: Immutable audit log
feature_items.description: Full history of every submission, field edit, and approval action with timestamp, user ID, and IP address. Cannot be altered retroactively.

feature_items.title: Data encryption at rest
feature_items.description: Submission data encrypted at the database level using AES-256.

feature_items.title: Invisible reCAPTCHA
feature_items.description: Spam and bot protection on public-facing forms without visible friction for real users.

feature_items.title: Password-protected forms
feature_items.description: Require a passcode to access a form URL — useful for internal forms shared externally.

feature_items.title: SSO / LDAP integration
feature_items.description: Authenticate form users against your corporate identity provider (Active Directory, Okta, etc.).

media.description: Screenshot of the form permissions panel showing role assignment grid with view/edit/approve checkboxes per role

sections.copy_notes: This section targets IT security reviewers and procurement teams, not end users. Lead with field-level permissions — this is deeper than what Typeform or standard Jotform plans offer. The immutable audit log is critical for compliance-heavy industries (finance, healthcare, government). Use trust-signal visual language: shields, locks, checkmarks — not abstract icons.

new_section_design_brief.layout: Split layout: left side shows a mobile phone mockup with a responsive form, right side shows a phone/IVR call flow diagram (caller → IVR → form questions → answers captured → data in platform). A bold 'IVA Exclusive' or 'Only on IVA' badge on the IVR side.
new_section_design_brief.visual_concept: Two sub-sections visually separated: (A) Mobile / Offline — standard responsive form on phone, (B) Voice / IVR — phone call flow showing questions delivered as audio and answers captured via keypad or voice. Use a horizontal divider or tab between the two.
new_section_design_brief.unique_selling_point: IVR voice form filling is completely unique to IVA — no western competitor (Typeform, Jotform, Fillout, Google Forms) offers this as a native form feature. This is the single most defensible differentiator on the entire page.
new_section_design_brief.tone: Matter-of-fact, not hypey. Show the real workflow: 500 customers need to fill a survey → auto-dialer calls all 500 → each answers via keypad → all responses land in the form dashboard. That's the story.

content.headline: Forms filled by phone call — not just a mobile browser
content.subheadline: Native IVR integration delivers form questions as an automated phone call and captures answers via keypad or voice recording. No smartphone required. No app to install.
content.body_copy: IVR voice form filling is available natively in IVA — not via a third-party integration. Deliver surveys, intake forms, complaint forms, and satisfaction questionnaires to any phone number. Combine with the auto-dialer module to run campaigns at scale: the system calls your list automatically, asks the questions, captures the answers, and populates the form dashboard in real time.

feature_items.title: IVR form delivery
feature_items.description: Form questions are played as voice prompts over an automated phone call. Respondents answer using their keypad (DTMF digits) or by recording a voice response for open-ended questions.

feature_items.title: Auto-dialer campaign
feature_items.description: Upload a contact list and the system automatically calls each contact to complete the form — no human agent required. Ideal for surveys, reminders, and data collection at scale.

feature_items.title: Manual IVR form entry
feature_items.description: Callers who reach your IVR menu can navigate to a form and fill it in during the call — useful for complaint registration, service requests, and satisfaction surveys.

feature_items.title: Offline web form
feature_items.description: Web forms continue to work offline on mobile devices. Responses are queued locally and synced to the server automatically when connectivity is restored.

feature_items.title: Responsive layout by default
feature_items.description: All forms auto-adapt to any screen size — phone, tablet, or desktop. No separate mobile version to maintain.

stats.label: Contacts reachable per auto-dialer campaign
stats.context: System calls all contacts automatically with zero manual effort

stats.label: Manual follow-up calls required
stats.context: The auto-dialer handles the entire outreach and data capture cycle

stats.label: Input modes supported
stats.context: Web browser, mobile (online/offline), and IVR voice call

voice_form_flow_diagram.description: Step-by-step flow showing: (1) Form designer creates form in IVA builder → (2) Auto-dialer calls contact list → (3) Respondent hears question via IVR → (4) Respondent presses keypad or speaks answer → (5) Answer captured → (6) Next question plays → (7) All answers land in form dashboard in real time
steps: Answer captured via keypad or voice
All responses stored in form dashboard

competitive_context.typeform: Web and mobile only — no IVR or voice capability
competitive_context.jotform: No native IVR integration — IVR would require custom middleware
competitive_context.fillout: Web only — no voice or IVR support
competitive_context.google_forms: Web only — no voice or IVR support
competitive_context.iva: Only platform with native IVR voice forms + auto-dialer + BPMS + web forms in a single product

media.description: Left: mobile phone showing a responsive multi-step form. Right: phone handset icon with an IVR call flow diagram showing question → keypad answer → next question → data captured in dashboard.

sections.copy_notes: This is the single strongest differentiator on the entire page. No western competitor has it. Do not bury this at the bottom — consider moving it to section 2 or 3 in the final page order if the target market includes government, utilities, or large enterprise with field workers. Keep the tone factual and workflow-focused — explain the exact scenario (500 customer survey via auto-dialer) rather than abstract claims.

page_order_recommendation.rationale: For enterprise/government audiences, move the Voice & Mobile section (10) to position 2 right after the hero — it is the most defensible differentiator and will immediately qualify the right buyers. For general B2B SaaS audiences, the current order (visual designer → fields → logic → steps → integrations → analytics → security → voice) follows a logical product discovery flow.

prefer: Specific numbers (30+ field types, not 'many field types')
Workflow descriptions (what the user does, not what the product is)
Outcome-first headlines ('Show only what's relevant' not 'Conditional Logic Feature')
Competitive specificity where accurate (name the exact limitation of a competitor)
Action verbs: build, define, connect, trigger, show, hide, route, embed
global_tone_guidelines.primary_audience: IT managers, operations leads, and digital transformation teams at mid-to-large organizations. Secondary: developers evaluating no-code vs custom build.
global_tone_guidelines.reading_level: Technical but not developer-only. Assume the reader knows what an API, webhook, and CRM are, but not what DTMF is.
